theScene: You return to the peak of the mountain using the checkpoint gem, materializing in the stone garden right outside of the Temple Dungeon, and after taking a few moments to prepare yourself, you push through its grand doors and walk inside...

As you enter into the dungeon, you notice that the interior of the building is just as ornately designed as the outside, with intricate and beautiful patterns adorning all of the walls, and an imposing set of heavily stylized wooden double doors with an impressive golden inlay dominates the entire wall in front of you.
To your left and right are two open doorways that lead to a decorated blue room and a decorated red room, respectively.
You notice a woman with fluffy fox ears poking around the doorway of the blue side, and as she turns around and sees you, she gives you a friendly wave before walking further into the room.
You stand in the center of the room, looking between the blue side of the dungeon, the red side, and the imposing double doors in front of you, deciding where to go.
Noting a part of the wall is ajar, you realize you could easily get to somewhere else in the temple if you really wanted to!
Go to the entrance of Aiko's chamber.
Go to the entrance of Feng's chamber.

EventText.NameOfScene: Go to the entrance of Aiko's chamber.
Go to the entrance of Feng's chamber.

theScene: You walk up to the massive set of doors and try to open them, but no matter how hard you pull, they refuse to budge even a single inch. They must be locked somehow...
With a shrug, you move back to the center of the room. You'll probably be able to get through them later, anyway.

You walk up to the massive set of doors and try to open them, but no matter how hard you pull, they refuse to budge even a single inch. You've already cleared the power trials, so you must need to clear the will trials as well to get them open.
With a shrug, you move back to the center of the room. You've already cleared one trial, so how hard can the other one be?
You try to open the huge doors, but they refuse to budge even an inch, you cleared the will trials, so you must need to clear the power trials to get the door open.
With a shrug, you move back to the center of the room. You've already cleared one trial, so how hard can the other one be?

You walk up to the massive set of doors and try to open them, and after several long moments of extreme effort, you manage to pull them open just enough to squeeze through them, and once you're on the other side you take a moment to absorb your surroundings.

You're standing on one end of a grand hallway, and a wooden pathway extends on the floor all the way to the other side with what appears to be a sparring ring at the halfway point and ending at a large flight of stairs that leads to an imposing set of doors at the top, and a series of large and intricately carved blue and red pillars line the left and right sides of the pathway respectively.

Upon closer inspection, you can see a large number of kunoichi trainees in a series of training and sparring areas, practicing on the left and right of the walkway through the pillars. While most of them seem to be practicing {i}actual{/i} combat techniques, quite a few are engaged in all kinds of lewd acts such as bondage play, sex toy usage, lesbian sex, and even more, but most of them seem too focused on what they're doing to notice your entrance.
(Note, the dungeon is not fully complete, and it's content currently ends here, the following scene will not play in builds once it is complete.)
You start to push open the large double doors, but they suddenly stop, a rather meek looking kunoichi trainee pokes her head out from the gap of the slightly open doors.
She looks back into the room she's in before looking back to you.
There was a incident in the training hall, and it's a complete mess... So um... Sorry, but you'll have to wait to continue the dungeon... I'm sorry!
She quickly closes the door on you in a panic. You can't help but feel like it was her fault... (Note: It is not the ninja girl's fault the dungeon isn't complete.)

EventText.NameOfScene: Go back to main hall from nin hall.
theScene: You exit the kunoichi training room and head back into the grand entryway of the dungeon, the large double doors slowly close shut behind you.
You decide to leave the dungeon and exit through the front doors before quickly returning to town using the checkpoint gem outside.

EventText.NameOfScene: Go back to the main hall.
theScene: You exit the red room and head back into the grand entryway of the dungeon.

EventText.NameOfScene: Go back to the main hall.
theScene: You exit the blue room and head back into the grand entryway of the dungeon.
You head through the open blue doorway into a room decorated with a sea of different blues and silvers.

There is a small door directly in front of you on the other side of room, and the fox eared woman you saw earlier walks through it just as you enter, turning briefly to beckon you to follow her before closing it behind her, and as it swings shut you notice that a stylized fox mask hangs from the front of it.

The other door in the room is to your right and is quite large, looking to be made from intricately designed silver, with the words 'Trials of Will' engraved on the wall just above it. The open door back to the main hall is behind you, and as you look back you can see into the red room on the other side of the dungeon as well.

The large, intricately designed silver door to your right draws your attention, and the words 'Trials of Will' are engraved on the wall just above it. There's a small door directly in front of you on the other side of the room that has a stylized fox mask hanging off a hook, and the open doorway back to the main hall is behind you, giving you a view not only of the main hall, but the red room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.
Go back to the main hall.
You carefully investigate the wall that Kotone told you the switch was hidden in, and after a few moments, you spot it!
As you pull it down, you hear a few muffled sighs of disappointment through the wall, and you're pretty sure that you won't encounter any more monsters on this side of the dungeon anymore.

You can't help but notice, however, that there was practically no resistance whatsoever when you actually pulled the switch. You get the feeling that it's not connected to any kind of mechanism at all...
As you pull it up, you hear a few muffled giggles through the walls, and you're pretty sure that you're going to encounter monsters on this side of the dungeon once more.

You can't help but notice, however, that there was practically no resistance whatsoever when you actually pulled the switch. You get the feeling that it's not connected to any kind of mechanism at all...
You walk up to the large door and push it open before walking further into the dungeon.
You're in a short hallway with another intricate silver door on the other end, and the words 'First Trial' are engraved with silver into the wall above it.
Go to the will trial entrance.

EventText.NameOfScene: Go to the will trial entrance.
theScene: You head back to the decorated entrance of the will trials, feeling your attention be instinctively drawn to the intricately designed silver door even though it's behind you. There is a small door to your right that has a stylized fox mask hanging off a hook, and the open doorway to the main hall is to your left, giving you a view not only of the main hall, but the red room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.
You head through the open red doorway and enter into a fancy looking room decorated with a wide variety of different reds and golds.
A large, intricately designed golden door draws your attention to your left, with the words 'Trials of Power' engraved on the wall above it.

Directly in front of you is a large, obviously bovine woman with a striking red kimono sitting in a chair who seems to be so engrossed with reading her book that she doesn't seem to realize that you're there. Through the open doorway behind you, you can see not only the main hall, but the blue room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.
A large, intricately designed golden door draws your attention to your left, with the words 'Trials of Power' engraved on the wall above it.

Directly in front of you is a familiar large and obviously bovine woman with a striking red kimono sitting in a chair who seems to be so engrossed with reading her book that she doesn't seem to realize that you're there. Through the open doorway behind you, you can see not only the main hall, but the blue room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.
Go back to the main hall.
You carefully investigate the wall that Minoni told you the button was hidden in, and after a few moments, you spot it!
As you press the button, you hear a few muffled sighs of disappointment through the wall, and you're pretty sure that you won't encounter any more monsters on this side of the dungeon anymore.

You can't help but notice, however, that there was practically no resistance whatsoever when you actually pressed the button. You get the feeling that it's not connected to any kind of mechanism at all...
As you press the button, you hear a few muffled giggles through the walls, and you're pretty sure that you're going to encounter monsters on this side of the dungeon once more.

You can't help but notice, however, that there was practically no resistance whatsoever when you actually pressed the button. You get the feeling that it's not connected to any kind of mechanism at all...
You walk up to the large door and push it open before walking further into the dungeon.
You're in a short hallway with another intricate golden door on the other end, and the words 'First Trial' are engraved with gold into the wall above it.

You head back to the decorated entrance to the power trials, feeling your attention be instinctively drawn to the intricately designed golden door, even though it's behind you. To your left, the large bovine woman is still reading her book, and seems just as oblivious to your presence as she did before. The open doorway to your right gives you a view not only of the main hall, but the blue room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.

You head back to the decorated entrance to the power trials, feeling your attention be instinctively drawn to the intricately designed golden door, even though it's behind you. To your left, the familiar bovine woman is still reading her book, and seems just as oblivious to your presence as she did before. The open doorway to your right gives you a view not only of the main hall, but the blue room on the other side of the dungeon as well.
Go back to the main hall.

You approach the reading woman, and as you near her she briefly glances up at you before looking back down at her book, then jerks her head back up at you with eyes wide, slamming her book shut and quickly hiding it within her kimono as she scrambles out of her chair.
O-Oh! H-Hello brave adventurer! Welcome to the... Shit, I already fucked up! Shit!

Her face flushes bright red with embarrassment as she frantically begins to pat her body all over as if looking for something before pulling out a small book from somewhere within her kimono, and she all but tears it open before starting to recite something written on one of the pages.
Uh, K-Kon... Kon... Ici... Wa? Kon'iciwa, Nǐ Hǎo, Hel- Wait just a second...
As her voice trails off, her eyes narrow with annoyance as she suddenly glares over your shoulder into the great hall, seeming to completely forget that you're even there.

...You were supposed to give me a list of things to say for my job, not to hand me a book on how to {i}greet{/i} people! 'Oh! I'll help the newbie', she says. 'I'll make sure she knows what to expect when she works here', she says! Why did I trust her?! AGAIN! {i}NOW{/i} what am I supposed to do when people come through-

She suddenly freezes as her focus returns to you, and her already flushed face turns an even darker shade of red as she closes her mouth abruptly, opens it and tries without success to speak, then closes it once more as she buries her face in her hands, letting her book to fall to the floor with a clatter as she squirms in embarrassment.

An awkward silence hangs in the air as the woman's face only continues to get redder and redder beneath her hands, and as it drags on and on, starting to become absolutely unbearable, you finally decide to introduce yourself.
Uh, hi there. My name's [ThePlayerName], and you are...?
She finally uncovers her face and gives you a look of wordless gratitude as she slowly takes a deep breath and flashes you a weak smile.
She suddenly takes in another deep breath, closing her eyes for a brief moment before straightening her spine and assuming a more dignified stance, even if her face is still quite red..

Hello, adventurer. I'm the proctor for your trials in the power side of this dungeon. My job is to inform you of the rules of the challenges, answer any questions you may have, and to make sure you don't try to cheat.
And you can call me... Ugh...
She lets out a quiet sigh of annoyance and bows her head slightly before looking back up at you with a slightly embarrassed expression.

Another moment of silence passes as you take a long look at her obviously bovine horns, her tail that keeps nervously flicking from side to side behind her, and her muscular physique, ending your inspection by staring her in the eyes with a raised eyebrow, feeling more than a little curious about the 'oni' part. But despite her obvious embarrassment as her face begins to flush once more, she refuses to say a single word more.
As you let out a sigh and drop the subject, she lets out a breath she was obviously holding and nervously glances over your shoulder once more as her expression tightens slightly.

A-Anyway... Just as a heads up... [ThePlayerName], was it? There's a fox woman on the will side of the dungeon, and even though she's the proctor, don't, uh, don't trust her, alright? She likes to play tricks on me, so she might do that to you as well. Just a suggestion.
As she returns to look at you once more, you notice that her face is almost completely back to its normal hue once more.

Oh, and I almost forgot to mention this, but once you head into the trials, I won't be able to talk about much other than what you have to do to beat the trial, and I won't help you beat it in any way. Soooo... Do you have any questions for me?

You approach the familiar woman as she continues to read, and as you near her she briefly glances up at you before looking back down at her book, then jerks her head back up at you with eyes wide, slamming her book shut and quickly hiding it within her kimono as she scrambles out of her chair.
U-Uh, h-hey again! W-Was there anything you needed?

You approach the familiar woman as she continues to read, and as you near her she briefly glances up at you before looking back down at her book, then jerks her head back up at you with eyes wide, slamming her book shut and quickly hiding it within her kimono as she scrambles out of her chair.
U-Uh, h-hey again [ThePlayerName]! W-What brings you back over here? You've already completed the power trials, so I'm not really sure why you're here though...
I wanted to spend some time with you.
I just had some questions about the trials.

EventText.NameOfScene: I wanted to spend some time with you.
theScene: Minoni's cheeks begin to turn slightly pink as she suddenly looks down at her feet.
As she looks back up once more, she seems to be deliberately avoiding your gaze and seems more than a little flustered by your words.

EventText.NameOfScene: I just had some questions about the trials.
theScene: You swear you saw her eyes droop ever so slightly from your words, but it was so subtle that you can't entirely be sure that you saw anything at all...
She awkwardly scratches her face with her index finger.

You approach the familiar woman as she continues to read, and as you near her she briefly glances up at you before looking back down at her book, then jerks her head back up at you with eyes wide, slamming her book shut and quickly hiding it within her kimono as she scrambles out of her chair.
U-Uh, hey there [ThePlayerName]! Is there something you wanted to talk about?

You approach the familiar woman as she continues to read, and as you near her she briefly glances up at you before looking back down at her book, then jerks her head back up at you as her face breaks into a grin, storing her book inside her kimono as she quickly stands up.
Hey there [ThePlayerName]! What brings you here?
Uh... Sure, I guess? I'm not sure what else you need to know about the trials considering you've already done them, but okay...

Minoni's face turns bright red as she opens her mouth, but doesn't seem able to utter even a single word as she angrily glares at you, and after forcing herself to take a deep breath, she finally manages to speak.
What?! No! Didn't I tell you I'm not like that, or were you not paying attention?!
She crosses her arms and turns away from you, and after a few minutes of trying to calm her down, she finally seems willing to talk to you once more, even if she seems a little more guarded now.
You can't help but notice, however, that she was blushing furiously the entire time, and even now her face is still slightly flushed as she looks at you.

Minoni's face turns a brilliant shade of crimson as she opens her mouth, seemingly unable to speak at all as she stares at you, and her blush deepens further as she tears her gaze away from you while looking off to the side.
Wh- Tha- You?! W-Why would you even- N-NO! S-Shut up! J-Just-

She clamps a hand over her mouth as she continues to adamantly avoid looking at you, and as you look away from her to give her a moment to recover her composure, you can't help but notice her stealing furtive glances in your direction...

As you continue to study the walls of the room, taking a second to admire the craftsmanship of the interior design of the dungeon, you hear a faint sigh and look over to see Minoni staring at you once more, and she lets out a girly 'eep' as your eyes meet hers.
C-Come on, [ThePlayerName], s-stop saying weird stuff like that! L-Let's just hang out o-or something...
She seems awfully keen to talk about something else...
Minoni flushes deeply at your suggestion and bashfully looks away before glancing back at you and giving you a quick nod.
...Come on, l-let's go to my room...
With blazing cheeks, she firmly grabs your hand and leads to you a wall, taking a moment to open up a hidden doorway before pulling you through.

Minoni leads you through several winding passageways, occasionally passing by a few kunoichi who make no attempts to conceal their giggling at the furiously blushing woman pulling you along, until you finally reach her cozy looking room.
As she pushes you into the room and firmly closes the door behind her, she looks over at you with an embarrassed, yet excited grin.
So... Did you have something particular in mind?
Isn't a minotaur a little out of place here?
So when are you going to try and fuck me?
She nods with an awkward smile, and as you turn to walk away she sits back down on her chair. As you reach the center of the room, she calls out to you one last time.
Oh! And good luck with the trials!
As you turn to look at her, she shyly waves at you before pulling her book out from her kimono and begins to read it once more.
Go back to the main hall.
Alright, uhm, have a good day [ThePlayerName].

After giving you a shy wave goodbye, you walk to the center of the room and decide what to do next, and you notice that Minoni has pulled her book out of her kimono and seems to have resumed her intense reading.
Yes. In case you didn't already know, both sides of the dungeon require you to go through a series of trials, and once you beat both of them you can continue on to the final part of the dungeon.
The trials on this side of the dungeon aren't very complex, so you shouldn't worry about them too much. You certainly look like you can handle yourself pretty well anyway, so you'll be fine!
She flashes you a cheerful grin, but flushes suddenly as she seems to think about what she just said, and shyly averts her eyes for a brief moment before looking back at you.

A-Anyway, the main goal is to progress through each of the rooms and pull the lever at the end to unlock the big door in the main hall. As I mentioned before, you'll need to pull the levers on both sides of the dungeon to open it.

But for this particular side there's going to be several tests of strength on the way, and once you reach the end you'll have to fight a challenging enemy to test your might. But don't worry about having to do it all at once, because any progress you make won't be reset.

If you can't finish one of the trials or want to do something else, you can just leave and come back whenever you want. You just have to be cautious of the monsters wandering around, because they'll attack you if they see you, but if you finish the trial they'll leave you alone. Well, except for the kunoichi, because they're a little different...
She takes a moment to glare at one of the nearby walls, and you swear you can hear the faint sounds of someone giggling from inside it.

...Anyway, that covers everything you'll need to complete the trials. For more details on individual challenges, you can talk to me in the dungeon, as I'll be in the room with you. Oh, and before I forget, there are a couple treasure chests you may find, and you're allowed to take whatever's inside them. It's not considered stealing.

EventText.NameOfScene: Isn't a minotaur a little out of place here?
theScene: She tenses up as her face goes red with embarrassment, but she forces herself to smile anyway.
Wh-What are you talking about? I'm obviously an oni! Just take a look at my outfit!
You just give her a level look without saying anything, and she fidgets for a moment before letting out a heavy sigh.

...O-Okay, yeah, I am a minotaur. Look, the boss of this dungeon wanted a certain kind of aesthetic for this place, and sometimes you have to make a few alterations to things to make everything fit, alright? Besides, it's easier to hire some of the positions locally, and the monsters who live around here are already used to living on mountains anyway.
Though... I'm the only minotaur that's been hired as far as I know... I hope I'm doing a good job at this...
She stares off into the distance with a look of mild concern...

She opens her mouth and looks as if she's going to defend herself, but stops with a faint look of irritation as she glances over your shoulder towards the large doors in the main hall before looking back at you.

...I'm not even going to try to argue about it. That isn't my real name, but for the purposes of me working here, just call me Minoni. My boss thought it was a good nickname for me, saying that it was a clever way to mix 'minotaur' and 'oni'.
Minoni grumbles and throws a dirty look over your shoulder towards the other side of the dungeon as she crosses her arms.
One of my coworkers is the proctor for the will side of the dungeon, and she likes to mess with me because I'm pretty new here. She usually says that she's just trying to help, but...
Her expression sours further as her eyes briefly become unfocused, lost in thought as she remembers some clearly irritating moments before she blinks and suddenly looks back at you.

She also has a tendency to fuc- uh, sorry, to {i}mess{/i} with people who take the trials. Wait... shouldn't they be called testees? No, uh... Adventurers. Yeah. We're technically not supposed to try to do anything funny with the adventurers, but she's told me before that she knows of a few loopholes or something.
Her face becomes serious as she glances over at the walls before leaning in closer to you before conspiratorially whispering at you.
...If I were you, I'd be careful about trusting her... {i}I{/i} really need to stop trusting her...
She suddenly seems to realize how close her face is to yours and jerks her head back with a girly sounding 'eep!', blushing slightly as she looks away from you.

EventText.NameOfScene: So when are you going to try and fuck me?
theScene: Minoni violently coughs and begins to sputter incoherently as her face turns a deep crimson shade.
{i}WH-WHAT{/i}?! My job isn't- I'm not- {i}NO{/i}! Why would you even say something like that?!
I would {i}never{/i} do something like that, and the trials of power don't end in sex if you fail! U-Uh, w-well the tests I manage, anyway! Th-The other monsters will probably... Uh... A-ANYWAYS!
Do you have any OTHER questions you wanted to ask me, that AREN'T about that?

She's still incredibly flustered as her face continues to flush a beet red color, and you think you heard her mutter something under her breath along the lines of 'I took this job to avoid that', but you can't really be certain...
I guess we can hang out and chat for a bit...
You're not like the other minotaurs I've met. You're a lot less... aggressive.
You're not like the other minotaurs I've met. You're a lot less... aggressive.
Ask to read her 'Lucidian Sweethearts!♥' manuscript.
Minoni immediately puts her hand over the part of her kimono where she stored her book, looking more than a little guilty as she nervously laughs at your question.
O-Oh, that? I-It's just- just a boring work related book! Y-Yeah...

She fidgets awkwardly as you stare at her, and you're almost positive that she's lying, but you decide not to press her any further. You get the feeling, however, that if she liked you more, she might tell you the truth.
But as she looks at your face, her expression falters, and she glances down at her hand as she slowly moves it away from her kimono.
U-Um, I-I mean if you really want to know, I-I can... Um...

She flushes with embarrassment as she slowly takes out the small book you saw her reading when you first walked up to her, and she holds it up to her chest as she nervously fidgets with the red cover.

I-I usually spend a lot of time reading b-because not a lot of people come through here, a-and I know it's not really a muscly minotaur kind of thing to do, but I like reading about the world, history, a-and... Well... P-Promise you won't laugh?
Minoni tentatively grabs the red cover of the book and, after a moment of hesitation, slides it off, revealing the actual garishly colorful cover to the book.

It takes you a handful of seconds to really comprehend what you're seeing, but after taking in the pink and pastel colors, the intricately patterned border of roses around the edges of the book, the group of excited and happy looking monster girls in the center, and the stylistic title {i}'Lucidian Sweethearts!♥'{/i} on the cover, you return your gaze to Minoni's face as she stares off at the wall with an embarrassed blush.

I-It's a romance novel from the east, a-and Kotone gave me one as a joke when I started working here, but I ended up really liking it a-and I'm on book 34 now! I think she said it was called manga, but... U-Um...
Minoni only gets more and more flustered as she desperately tries to look at anything other than you in the room.

I-It's a story about finding true love, shown between the perspective of a few different monster girls over the course of several books, with d-dates, and stuff like that! It's about normal relationships instead of the usual... U-Um... 'Ravish the guy until he loves you' motif. A-And it's not even an erotic novel!
She finally seems to muster up the courage to look you in the face, even if she's blushing profusely as she does so.

I-I've always been interested in these kinds of books, e-even the really trashy romance novels that sometimes come out of the capital, b-but whenever I tried to talk to any of the other minotaurs about this stuff, t-they just made fun of me for it, so...
Chat with her about the book.

EventText.NameOfScene: Chat with her about the book.
theScene: As you ask Minoni a few questions you had about the book, she seems genuinely surprised that you're taking this seriously, and after a couple minutes of talking she begins to ease into the conversation, relaxing as she excitedly tells you about some of the overarching plot arcs and the main characters, as well as which ones are her favorites.

She refuses to get into any spoilers, however, stating that she's doing it 'in case you want to read them yourself', and it's obvious that Minoni is extremely happy to finally have someone to talk with seriously about this. As the conversation eventually winds down, you notice that she's can't seem to stop herself from smiling.
You can hardly stifle a laugh at the prospect of a big strong minotaur like her reading trashy romance novels, and you can't help yourself as you poke some fun at her hobby.

But your words seem to have cut deeper than you intended as her face immediately twists into a look of betrayal, and she refuses to talk to you or even look at you for a long while, glaring coldly at the walls as you fruitlessly try to start the conversation over, and only after a few minutes does she begrudgingly start to talk to you again...
Minoni scrunches up her face in anger and quickly stows the book back in her kimono as she lets the topic drop. An awkward silence fills the air as you try to think about what to say talk about...

EventText.NameOfScene: You're not like the other minotaurs I've met. You're a lot less... aggressive.
theScene: Minoni suddenly coughs as she blushes fierce, clearly caught off guard by your bluntness.
I... W-Well, I guess that's one way of putting it...
She gives you a serious look, even as she continues to flush at your words.
But it's good for you to know that not every monster girl wants to just pin down and fuck every guy she meets, you know. Emotional needs are important too!

Even for minotaurs like me, really. We tend to have really high libidos, and speaking from personal experience I can say it's like being in heat all year round, but that's no excuse for neglecting... U-Um...
She trails off as she seems to realize what she just said, and she averts her gaze as she sputters with embarrassment at the somewhat personal information she just gave out.

Th-The point is! That it's- uhm, possible to suppress natural instincts and redirect your focus on other things. I personally use a mixture of herbal teas and meditation, and while I could do without them, it makes things a lot easier.
I-I just enjoy being able to think clearly, and it's fun to enjoy things other than sex.

O-Oh! But I'm not saying that minotaurs are all 'pin you down and fuck you' all the time, just that once they've had their- uhm, I mean once they've spent some time with their mate, which usually ends up being some random guy, they generally cool off and can be quite reasonable.
She idly runs one of her hands through her hair, then stops as she stares at a stray strand that hangs in front of her face with a contemplative look.

And technically now that I think about it, I'm actually a subspecies of minotaur... Or would a genetic mutation be more accurate? Either way, the color red usually spikes arousal in minotaurs, but not for me. Thankfully, the color of my hair doesn't seem to affect other minotaurs, because I can imagine that being... Difficult to deal with.
She lets out a rueful giggle as she straightens out her hair, then takes another look at the red walls of the room with a concerned, thoughtful look.
Although... Now that I think about it, if any other minotaurs tried to work here, they'd have a much harder time containing their... Um... Urges.

Why not? It's a steady paying job, I get free clothes, free room and board, books to read, and best of all, I don't have to scavenge around the mountain to get things like that anymore. I can't think of any reason why I'd want to leave.
But almost immediately after saying that, her eyes go wide as she suddenly remembers something and she flushes as she stares down at the ground.
...W-Well, except for the all the sex, but you kind of have to deal with that everywhere, s-so...

B-But other than that, this place is pretty great. And even if there were more downsides, being here still beats trying to haggle with harpies. They might look cute, but some of them drive some really hard bargains...
Minoni flashes you a cocky grin as she leans in closer to you while sizing up your arms.

She playfully saunters closer as she speaks, not hiding her curiosity as she cranes her neck to have a look at the bundle in your hands.

The succubus' smirk becomes more lopsided as you show her the manuscript's first page to explain, though she somehow doesn't appear that interested in the end, only giving it a perfunctory glance before her gaze returns to you and she mockingly clicks her tongue.
Tsk tsk, giving in so easily... How indiscreet of you, [ThePlayerName]. What would our poor proctor say if she knew?~

The succubus doesn't hide her annoyance as you protectively keep the collection of sheets against your torso in order to keep their contents concealed from her gaze, your gesture prompting her to tetchily huff.
Hmph, spoilsport... Well, fine. Have it your way.

Before you can react to that, however, she seems to have already moved on, trading her demonstrative expression for a contrastingly easygoing smile as she nimbly steps back, idly rocking on her heels and nodding at the penned texts as she volunteers.
No need to try and look surprised or deny it, [ThePlayerName]. Hehe.~ It's part of a kunoichi's job to know about people's secrets.♪

... That, and also, that girl really needs to get better at the whole 'inner voice' thing. She's {i}way{/i} too into the habit of speaking her thoughts aloud, especially when she thinks there's no one around... I'm pretty sure I could tell you the whole plot from start to finish, and {i}that{/i} is without me having even put in any effort into eavesdropping...

While she crosses her arms and shakes her head knowingly, you reflect that it's all well and good, but it still doesn't let you see where exactly she's going with that unprompted visit and her ensuing soliloquy, though as if to answer that question, she turns back to you and looks at you with a surprisingly genuine-feeling smile as she goes on.

But anyway! I just wanted to say: good on her for trying, and best of luck to her with the whole submission thing! That story of hers sounds like it's got potential, so I'm looking forward to reading it once it makes it to the books... Pretty sure that she'd freak out if I approached her about it, though, so you'll be the middleman... Don't actually {i}tell her{/i} I told you, but do your best to support her, 'kay?
She gives you a wink, then looks like she's about to take her leave, having seemingly said her piece, and now appearing ready to just up and depart as breezily as she arrived.
Great.♪ I knew I could count on you.
The ninja lady pouts emphatically at the idea, but then just shrugs as she looks back to you with an air of plain disappointment.
I mean... You can if you want to? No skin off my nose... But I'm pretty sure it would only make her spaz out. Make of that what you will.
The notion actually seems to amuse her, the lively demonette giving out a rueful chortle at your direct accusation.
Honestly, 'stalker' is such an ugly word... Let's just say instead that I'm a distant fan who's supporting her from the shadows while practicing her skills as a kunoichi.~

And with that, she suddenly grabs a little ball hanging from her waist and throws it to the ground, conjuring a brief burst of smoke that lingers just long enough for her to abscond, the obfuscating vapor soon vanishing away to let you see that your surroundings are now devoid of kunoichis... Visible ones, at least.

You're not sure that kind of exit was necessary, and possibly even less sure of what that was all about... but with you being now back alone and done with your reading, you get up from your chair and idly shuffle the ream of literary papers back into an orderly pile, wondering if you should go and look for Minoni.

... Only for the lady herself to return as if on cue, her muscular frame contrastingly oozing shy trepidation as she gives you a fidgety glance, before stammeringly piping up when she sees you seem to be done going through her material.
S-So, what did you th-think, [ThePlayerName]...? I-I'm open to criticism!
Despite plainly being a nervous wreck, she seems sincere when she says it, a look of unquestionable determination showing in her eyes as she awaits your feedback.
Minoni instantly stiffens when you bring up the topic, her gaze sharpening into a downright intense expression of mixed trepidation and eagerness as she tersely prompts.
Though she then immediately does a double take, looking around with a bit of a fluster, as if to make sure no one is listening in before adding.
A-Actually, I should go and get it, first. Just wait a few minutes.

And a few minutes are indeed all that the eager authoress needs to speedily go to her quarters, then return with her precious manuscript, which she hands to you before fixing you with an almost intimidating waiting stare, the atmosphere suddenly becoming studiously serious as she immediately goes into author mode.

As you're about to broach the topic of Minoni's manuscript, you remember that the last time she told you about her progress, she was still stuck on the climactic erotic scene between her story's two main characters.

She mentioned not managing to handle it well, due to lacking a {i}practical{/i} example of what such a moment would be like. This is where having a romantic partner would come in handy, but that is presently not the case between you and her... and at the same time, you're pretty sure that if she'd met someone like that, she'd have told you about it.

With how skittish the minotaur is, you're rather certain that a head-on proposition in that regard would just turn her into a flustered mess... but considering how reading seems to bring out the more liberated parts of her, you feel like it could possibly be a good way to indirectly approach the topic and make progress on that front?
For the time being, however, you deem it more prudent not to actually voice anything, lest you stoke her anxieties on the subject. Better to leave it be until new developments occur...

Remembering that Minoni was interested in having someone with whom to put the [TPN]aron & Clea scene into practice, you bring up the topic, since the two of you are an item, which {i}presumably{/i} matches what she had in mind.
And while the idea initially leaves her deeply flushed and stuttering in shyness, she quickly recovers and nods, albeit somewhat stiffly.
Y-Yes, that's... definitely something I'd like the two of us to... umh, {i}experiment{/i} with...
B-But talking about it right in the middle of the Temple's public areas feels {i}way{/i} too embarrassing... s-so if you're up for it, ask me when we're in my room, and we can... give it a shot?

Despite her apparent meekness, you can tell that she's definitely interested in the idea, the red-haired monster girl looking at you with thinly veiled eagerness while her tail flicks impatiently behind her, making it feel like she's just waiting for you to say the word.
She lets out a heartfelt sigh at that, then shrugs with a hint of moodiness before answering.
Still nothing... I know they're busy, though, so I'm not getting my tail in a twist about it... Or at least, I'm {i}trying{/i}.
B-But it's still kinda nerve-wracking to be waiting and waiting, knowing that the fate of my story is in the hands of people I've never met in my life...
Though I know that whether I make the cut or not, I'll be informed, since they'll send me a letter about it...
... p-possibly along with my manuscript if it got rejected, b-but I'd rather not think about it, or I feel like I'll start getting nightmares about it... Gah-
Clicking her tongue in self-reproach at getting carried away into her own insecurities again, she winces, then inhales deeply to calm herself down before concluding.
But yeah, until then, nothing to do but wait.

She stresses her words with another shrug, clearly not too comfortable with the situation, but also clearly having no other choice but to put up with it. Her eyes then go back to you with a little smile as she waits for what else you've come to her about, possibly hoping that you'll help her take her mind off all the waiting.
The monster girl writer shows a bright grin at your line of inquiry, all of her previous anxiety on the subject definitely looking to be a thing of the past as she readily answers.

Oh, it's been going {i}really{/i} well! I've been in regular correspondence with that editor they hooked me up with, and we've been getting along like a house on fire! Their feedback is {i}excellent{/i}, and I just need to read through it for new ideas to immediately start flowing!

Almost dancing in place in her excited state, the giddy minotaur looks like she might be about to launch into a whole gushing rant about her authorly doings, though she simmers down a tad as she seems to notice that, her peppy expression returning to a more composed but still enthusiastically beaming smile while she goes on more soberly.
O-Of course, an editing pass over a whole book is long and arduous, so we won't be done anytime soon... but we'll get there eventually. And the result will be better than ever, you'll see!
And with that confident claim, it seems like it's all she has to say on the current and quite positive state of her affairs, until she then turns pensive as she muses.

Though... maybe I'm just getting ahead of myself, but it really {i}is{/i} a bit crazy how much that editor and I have been gelling. It's already like we've known each other for months, and something about the way they write feels weirdly familiar, too...

Her face a tableau of perplexity, she chews on that for a bit, the notion appearing to have been niggling at her, although she quickly dismisses it with a shake of her head when she realizes she's probably just spinning in circles, letting out a scoff of mild self-reproach as she resolutely declares.

But that's definitely me reading too much into it. That must just be because I've read a lot of {i}Lucidian Sweethearts{/i} stuff, so exchanging with a member of their staff is... known territory, you know.
She shrugs at that, looking like she won't be getting any grey hairs over that one suspicion, and concluding with a tender smile.
So yeah, it's all been going great... And I have you to thank for it, [ThePlayerName].♪ So once more, thank you so very much for supporting me when I needed it.♥
Okay... Looks like I'm not much closer to overcoming that writer's block, then...
N-Not that I'm blaming you or anything! You're already helping me plenty!
F-For now, I'll just go and put the manuscript back in my room before anyone ends up catching us, but if you think you've got more for me later, just let me know!

In no time flat, the energetic woman has made the trip back and forth, which brings the two of you back on your own and with nothing in particular to move on to, resulting in Minoni giving you a silent questioning look as she waits to see if there's anything else you wanted from her.
Minoni instantly deflates at the expected and dreaded question, the big woman letting out a heavy sigh while her ears droop guiltily.
I... still haven't written it yet.

I {i}genuinely{/i} feel like [TPN]aron and Clea overcoming their reservations and acknowledging their attraction towards each other through no-frills sex makes for a satisfying climax... No pun intended.

But whenever I get to that part, I get writer's block... It's like... all of my style's shortcomings come back in force to haunt me, and there's nothing I can do but stare at the page with nothing coming to mind.
Again, she sighs, but then returns her gaze to you with spirited ardor, definitely looking like she's not willing to throw in the towel, and raising an arm as if in a show of combat readiness.

S-So... if you've got any input on what kind of issues my writing has, and what I could do to overcome them, I really feel like that could help 'unstick' me... So go ahead and lay it on me, [ThePlayerName]!

Her fiery plea ending in a ferocious shout that dramatically reverberates and echoes through the Temple's vacant corridors, her stare bores into you with fierce insistence, actually making you blink in slightly shaken surprise at the suddenly intense display of authorly motivation.
... Which she soon becomes aware of, the realization making her stumble back and cough awkwardly, her arm stiffly returning to her side while she sputters.
A-Ah, uhm, but... i-it's not like it's a {i}huge deal{/i}, s-so... no pressure, okay?
You can't help but feel like she's one to speak as she nonetheless returns to a less gung-ho attitude, quietly waiting for your critique.

For a moment, she seems a bit disappointed that you couldn't be the source of the breakthrough she was hoping for, but obviously not wanting to put that on you, she takes it on the chin and replies evenly.
Gotcha... I can't help but feel like there's still something amiss, though, although I can't put my finger on it...
Well, thanks for being there for me, at least. That really means something to me.
A short silence follows the simple heartfelt declaration, the lull prompting her to add with a slightly awkward nod at the bundle of pages still in your hands.
S-So... anything else? Or should I take this back for now?

As you've been reading through Minoni's story, you've noticed a number of grammatical and stylistic issues: a typo here, a run-on sentence there, a slight problem with reusing the same terms or turns of phrase too often... All in all, nothing critical, but still something that can really take one out of the narrative, especially when, for instance, she happens to absentmindedly misspell 'lip' as 'leap'...

Nodding as you deliver your feedback and as you helpfully annotate her manuscript with fixes and indications, she seems to be taking it all to heart, and to genuinely not be demotivated by your somewhat lecturing corrections as you might have feared, instead thoughtfully pondering.
Mh, I get what you mean... It might not seem like much, but I do guess it's the kind of thing that can make a difference between an okay story and a good story...
Thanks, [ThePlayerName]! That'll definitely help! And I'll make sure to give it a solid editing pass to address it all.
It truly appears that your input has done its job of prodding her onward, giving her inspiration and motivation for her next writing forays, just as she was hoping.
The sensitive minotaur wilts a bit at the topic, seeming to have been fearing that you'd bring up something like that.
D-Do you mean that it's not... appealing enough?
Comfortingly, you explain that it's not that, but instead that her style can oftentimes feel oddly... restrained?

As examples, you refer her to several descriptions of the two protagonists where, despite the intent clearly being to portray their sensuality and erotic appeal, the vocabulary and phrasing that she employs come off as strangely allusive and lukewarm, as if she'd purposefully kept herself from expressing her sincere feelings as a writer.

And it seems that you've hit the proverbial nail on the head, as Minoni blushes more and more fiercely as you elaborate, the aspiring authoress looking like the topic and choice excerpts are making something of an impression on her, ultimately prompting her to shyly stutter.
Ha... Haha, y-yeah... I can actually see what you mean... I guess I did kinda unconsciously... censor myself?
I-I'll definitely have to give those parts a second pass, b-but... not right now. I'll need to be... {i}alone{/i} for that.

Seeing the kind of face she's making, you can't but wonder about what her writing method is like, though you feel that it would probably be a tad too nosy to ask, especially considering how flustered Minoni can get on the subject... Better to move on for now.

A common pitfall of writers is to be too hasty in getting to the parts they're looking forward to, and so to gloss over the more 'boring' bits in the process, which ends up making said bits feel obligatory, and ultimately stands as a detriment to the eventual payoff... And for all her qualities as an author, Minoni has definitely been guilty of this, which you directly point out with examples to back your claims.
And while she clearly doesn't look happy about that, your horned friend doesn't seem to be making light of your input either, mostly seeming angry at herself as she mutters.
Urgh, yeah... Now that you mention it, those parts definitely come across as... cumbersome... I'll have to work on that.
Mmh... In fact, just talking about it is giving me a few ideas, so... thanks for acting as a sounding board, [ThePlayerName]!
The aspiring penwoman takes that strange feedback in with undisguised perplexity, looking quite unsure of what you're getting at, and repeating the word as if trying to make sense of it.

With her looking at you like she's downright wondering if you're straight-up bullshitting her, you hurry and elaborate, explaining that all good stories need some unforgettable key scenes, some memorable quotes and zingers... Parts that will stick to the reader's mind and leave an impression on them!

And while Minoni still looks a bit doubtful, her expression lights up as you bring up a few moments that she could further stress, spruce up, or highlight, her skepticism gradually melting away as she seems to warm up to the idea, a smile eventually showing on her face as she nods pensively.
Huh... You know what? I think I get it... It's a bit hard to explain, but I can already think of a few ways I could do just that! This should be fun.♪

She hums happily as she muses on the ways she could give some extra 'zing' to her story, the prospect looking to have done a fine job of resparking her imagination... which is a good thing, because you actually {i}were{/i} making it up as you went, which, in retrospect, could have really gone poorly...

As you look back to the manuscript and try to think of what else you could say about it that could help Minoni, it actually appears that her gears are doing some work of their own, the minotaur visibly mulling over what you just said and muttering under her breath in deep focus, until she pipes up.
Dramatic silence follows the somewhat timid declaration, the red-haired woman staring quietly at you as if to savor the moment, before finally exulting.
{b}I got it!{/b} I know what to do, now! The ideas are flowing!

She exuberantly declares while giddily jumping in place, the energetic motion causing quite the ruckus as her weighty hooves repeatedly slam into the floor... and also as her loosely constrained breasts jump within the slack embrace of her kimono, presenting you with quite the alluring view...

However, you're not given much time to appreciate their bouncing show, as they are soon pressed into your face by the muscular monster girl who unexpectedly catches you in a bear hug and shakes you around!
Thank you!!! Thank you {i}so much{/i}, [ThePlayerName]! You're a lifesaver!

You're not sure you've ever seen her so innocently excited, the jubilant horned lady not seeming to realize that she's just treated you to a bone-crushing motorboating session as she eventually lets go of you, though she still holds your slightly woozy self by the shoulders as she goes on with a fervid smile.
I can't wait to get to it! Leave it up to me, [ThePlayerName]: by the time you next swing by, I'll have it all ready for you to finish reading!
And in fact, without waiting for you to respond, she continues to ride her gleeful high and authoritatively pushes you out of the premises...

... right out of the Temple's doors where she happily waves you off before hurriedly returning inside with nary another word, looking far too eager to get back to her writing endeavors to delay their continuation a moment longer...
Looks like you'll have to 'swing by' some time or other, then? But for the moment...

As you approach Minoni, you find her with her eyes riveted to her book, which is not unusual... But what {i}does{/i} seem out of the ordinary are the creases of frustration that show across her forehead, the red-haired woman muttering unintelligibly as she pores over the pages in front of her, seeming to be looking at them without actually reading them, obviously deeply preoccupied with something.

And unlike her usual habit of being surprised by your presence, she doesn't even flinch when you come into view, instead neatly stashing her book into her kimono before fixing you with an intense gaze and declaring without preamble.

You could somewhat figure that her unordinary attitude was related to that, though you would have expected her to be more enthusiastic about it... But before you can inquire on the subject, she carries on on her own, her expression at least becoming a smidge less stiff as she grumbles.

And with your help, it's really been coming together better... but I still haven't been able to tackle the... the final scene between [TPN]aron and Clea... Y-You know, the one where they... {i}become intimate{/i}.

Her attitude suddenly changing from discontent huffing to an at least more familiar expression of self-conscious demureness, she hesitates a bit before carrying on, shyly tapping her fingers together without daring to look directly at you.
Uhm... As you're aware, I... get flustered easily... s-so whenever I think about what that moment should be like, I... lose my composure and end up unable to write anything...
I... Uhm, I-I think that if I could... {i}practice{/i} it i-in person, that might help...
B-But I don't have someone like that in my life, s-so... I've been left... at a standstill...
An awkward silence hangs in the air at the confession, leaving you unsure of what to say, the two of you staring at each other until Minoni ends up sputtering.
{b}A-Anyway{/b}, that's not your problem to deal with, s-so... let's just leave it at that!
You've already been helping me quite a bit, [ThePlayerName], so once more, thank you, and... let's talk about something else.

Looks like no matter how comfortable she is with you, Minoni is not ready to consider the two of you close enough to 'become intimate'... And knowing her, proposing it straight-up would send her into a stuttering panic... But maybe that reading, the very hobby that brought about this whole thing in the first place, would be a way to bridge the gap? The shy minotaur does seem to get more uninhibited when she's deep in an engaging story...
... W-Which is where you cum- {i}come{/i} in, [ThePlayerName]. Y-You could play the part of [TPN]aron, a-and help me... simulate that scene, f-for inspiration...
N-N-Not that you {b}have{/i} to...! I-It's only if you want to... I-In order to help me, a-and...
The more she goes on, the more incoherent the poor girl is becoming, her speech gradually devolving into a mess of undecipherable squeaky stutters, until she eventually blurts out.
{b}A-Anyway{/b}, th-there you have it! I-If you want to h-help, let me know next time we're in my room... A-And if not, {b}n-no big deal{/b}!

Arms flailing and hooves trampling in place, the panicking minotaur finishes delivering her request with a bellow that certainly makes it sound like a {i}little{/i} more than 'no big deal', though she then makes a visible effort to gather herself, breathing deeply and rhythmically to chase away her agitation, and eventually letting out a loud calming exhale before concluding.
N-Now... let's talk about something else before I die of embarrassment...
When you approach Minoni, you find her seemingly more alert than usual, the red-haired lady immediately noticing your presence and raising her eyes to you while leaving her book aside...
... and looking quite chipper as she takes you in, greeting you with a note of surprisingly carefree enthusiasm in her voice.

To get the obvious out of the way, since I imagine you must be wondering: yup, I finished my manuscript! The final push to wrap it up was honestly kinda grueling, but it's {i}done{/i}: completed, proofread, finalized, and sent to the publisher for review! Now, all that's left to do is wait for their response...

Judging by the heavy sigh of relief that she then breathes out, it seems that having finalized the whole writing affair has taken quite the weight off her shoulders, the minotaur closing her eyes to bask in the pleasant feeling.
... Only to soon open them again with a growing shadow of doubt in her gaze, a familiar look of flustered anxiety visible on her face as she stammers.
Th-Though... now that I think about it, h-having to wait for an answer about my work may actually... be {i}worse{/i} than not managing to complete it? G-Gosh, now I'm going to be a mess about-
N-No! I need to get a grip on myself! I've done my best, and that's what matters!

Slapping her cheeks with loud claps of her muscular hands that would probably give half a concussion to a regular human being, she reins in her distress with a conscious effort, then looks back to you with a nod of determination.
Yeah... It's out of my hands, now. All I can do is wait, and hope for the best.
A-And also, I should thank you, [ThePlayerName]... You played a big part in helping me complete my manuscript, so... Thank you. Thank you very much.

A warm, intimate moment passes as she fixes you with affectionate gratitude, though all too soon, she then goes back to sputtering awkwardly and looking away uncomfortably as something else comes back to her.

O-Oh, and also... Sorry for kinda... forgetting about you last time? I was really in the zone, and... I ended up tuning everything else out... But I know it's not an excuse for neglecting you, so... sorry, really!
'It's okay, you can make it up to me with more sex.'
I know... And I also know it's a bit late to say it, but again: sorry... And I understand if you're annoyed.

The minotaur's cow ears droop sadly while she doesn't dare to look at you directly, quickly creating a heavy, awkward silence between the two of you, and so leaving it up to you to now carry on with the conversation if you so desire, Minoni not looking like she's feeling very chatty right now, after her succession of high and low moods.

She still looks to be feeling guilty about that blunder of hers, but at least seems to be taking your word for it, your no-frills reassurance quieting her nerves and leaving her in a better mood as she nonetheless proceeds to stay silent, apparently not having anything else to add on the subject, and now waiting for your cue to keep the conversation going.
She immediately sputters at your unexpected response, her face instantly turning a fetching crimson while she squeaks out.

{i}E-Exciting!?{/i} Wh-What do you even mean by that? {i}What part?!{/i} Seeing me get inspired? Being neglected right after sex? Watching me write?! ... Actually, you know what, {i}don't answer that!!!{/i}

Pouting emphatically at your brazen display of kinkiness, she looks aside while her tail whips around in a tizzy, leaving her stewing in her own confusion and embarrassment, though apparently without any actual animosity, her voice not holding much venom at all as she peevishly mutters.

The big lady lets out a little sound of surprise as you go and claim her lips in lieu of response, but she promptly yields to your affections and embraces you to return them in kind with a happy sigh, the two of you partaking of a gentle smooch that nevertheless quickly turns into a rather heated makeout session as you keep going... until Minoni proves reasonable and breaks the liplock with a playful giggle.
But we're still in a semi-public place, and I'm still at work, so... let's keep the rest for when we're in my room, okay?♥︎
With a loving parting caress, she breaks the hug, then adds no more, waiting for you to say if you wanted anything else from her.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'It's okay, you can make it up to me with more sex.'
theScene: Caught by surprise, the proctor sputters a bit at your straightforwardly raunchy answer, but quickly recovers, scoffing at you with nonetheless no ire in her tone as she snaps back.
She sneakily adds before softly giggling at the naughty promise, then looking at you quietly, waiting to see if there's something else you came to her for.

As soon as you approach Minoni, you can tell that something is amiss: the minotaur girl is in an obvious low mood, her brow furrowed and her mouth set in a sad droopy pout, while her eyes seem to be staring ahead at the book in her hand without actually reading the words therein.

And when she takes you in, her expression shifts to something part reluctance, part relief, her stance relaxing ever so slightly as she shuffles uneasily in place, seeming uncustomarily unsure of how to greet you, and eventually volunteering in a worryingly small voice.

A rather uneasy silence ensues as her reading arm droops lamely while she bashfully looks aside, giving you the feeling that she has something to get off her chest, but doesn't dare bring it up. For a moment, you wonder if she's waiting for you to prod her on the subject or if she straight-up doesn't feel like talking, until she seems to finally muster the resolve to look you in the eye and speak up.
I... got a response about my manuscript.

Without another word, she stashes her book, and produces a neatly wrapped, yet-unopened parcel which looks far too big to be a simple letter. In fact, it appears to be just the size of Minoni's manuscript. And if said manuscript was returned to her, then it must mean that...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Sense Of Loss (2_04) 54bpm.mp3
Looks like I didn't make the cut.

Letting out a heart-wrenching sigh, the brawny bookworm goes quiet once more as she lets the news sink in, her morose speechlessness giving you a measure of how hopeful she was about the whole venture... and also lending you an opportunity to chime in, the aspiring authoress certainly looking like she could do with some commiseration.

For a moment, your positive comment brings a sincere smile to the minotaur's face, your unprompted testimony of staunch support seeming to cheer her up a bit, though her expression then quickly returns to a crestfallen one as she sadly nods.
Yeah, I thought so, too... But not good enough, apparently.

Your vehement exclamation immediately snaps Minoni out of her gloom, the horned lady having clearly not expected you to have such {i}intense{/i} feelings to voice, and sputtering in confusion for a moment before she stammeringly volunteers.

D-Don't say that! I-I'm sure they gave it a very fair review, and... they get a lot of applications, you know! It makes sense that they can't accept everyone, and that they have stringent standards...

Despite her shocked words and litany of excuses on the behalf of the publishing house, she can't seem to hold back what looks like a flicker of genuine cathartic satisfaction at your sympathetic outburst, her still blushing face showing a timid but genuine-feeling smile that proves to be a pleasant contrast to her previous glumness.

The minotaur looks like she'd like to share your confidence, but also like she doesn't want to be too hopeful and fall headfirst into cruel disappointment either, her self-conscious hesitation eventually giving way to cautious optimism as she answers with the shadow of a guarded smile.
Hm, {i}maybe{/i}... That sure would be a nice surprise...

The muscly woman lets out a rather cute sound of surprise as you affectionately embrace her in lieu of a response, her large body briefly tensing before quickly relaxing as she leans into the gesture, wrapping her own arms around you to give you a gentle appreciative squeeze, the big girl looking like she needed that.

For a nice little moment, the two of you softly nuzzle in silence, simply enjoying each other's comforting presence until you step away from each other, the red-haired monster girl showing you a sweet little smile as she tenderly offers.

However, her seriousness soon returns as she responsibly refocuses her attention on the package in her hand which she brings in front of her face with a fatalistic look, taking a long, deep breath in an effort to steady her nerves before speaking up again with grim resolve.
Well, nothing to do but open it and see what their response is.
... Which I could have done on my own, but since you contributed to my manuscript to such a meaningful extent, I wanted you to be there for it.
A-And also... I admit I couldn't muster the nerve to do it without you around... Even though I know it's not like it's going to change anything...

Realizing that she's just rambling and delaying the inevitable at this point, she gives a little 'Hmph...' of self-dissatisfaction, and moves on to the moment of truth by somewhat brusquely tearing off the packing paper, revealing a carefully stacked ream of paper that looks furiously familiar, and whose sight seems to make its author's heart further sink.

However, you both soon notice that it's not all there is to the package: a clean page, covered with artfully calligraphed inken writing, has been put at the top of the pile, the sight making Minoni circumspectly frown with a tetchy mutter.
Well, at least, they bothered to write back.

Notwithstanding her clear frustration and continued poor mood, you can tell that she's keenly interested in seeing what kind of feedback she got, a hint of trepidatious excitement showing on her face as she reads through the elegantly couched lines with you by her side.

{i}However, we do consider the skillfulness of your writing and the substance of your story to have the potential to be fitting additions to the series.
Therefore, should you be interested in further pursuing the possibility of your work getting published by us, we encourage you to renew your efforts and to recontact us with a revised version of your manuscript, which we will make sure to examine with due attention.{/i}

{i}To that effect, please find herewith our editing recommendations under the form of general stylistic and narrative feedback, as well as in-detail notes attached to your manuscript.
With our most obliged gratitude for your appreciation towards 'Lucidian Sweethearts!♥' and your motivation in contributing to it, we look forward to your response, and wish you luck on your continued writing endeavors.{/i}

For a moment, a stunned silence follows as Minoni remains frozen in place, staring at the letter in front of her as if she couldn't believe the words... Then, slowly, her brain seems to reconnect, her body nervously trembling while her breathing quickens and her lips quiver in a hesitant stutter.

Jubilating all at once, she thrusts up her arms in exultation, then, before you even have the time to react to her cheerful outburst, she suddenly wraps her arms around you and sways happily with you in her tight embrace, the musclebound authoress casually twirling you around as if you barely weighed a thing, the resulting frantic spinning proving no less dizzying than her exuberant joy as she merrily shouts.
[ThePlayerName], it's not over! I still have a shot! {b}I can make it!{/b}

Celebrating without restraint, she laughs against you while continuing to dance with you and jump around with so much unbound energy that you fear she's going to break something... including possibly you as you're getting hugged and shaken around with the kind of nonchalant overpowering strength that only a minotaur could muster!

But thankfully, before it actually gets to bodily injury or property damage, she realizes that she's getting a {i}bit{/i} carried away and lets go of you, carefully setting you down while looking aside and awkwardly clearing her throat as she lets you recover from that rather overwhelming display of enthusiasm on her part, your woozy form teetering in place before the embarrassed writer as you struggle to regain your balance.
Erm... Sorry, I couldn't help myself, ha ha...

Closing her eyes, she takes a moment to come back down from her cloud nine, though she can't keep a giddy smile off her face even as she puts her martial practice to good use by professionally re-exerting control over herself, her gleeful breaths quickly and smoothly evening back to a steady respiration, leaving her more companionably composed as she reopens her eyes and goes on in a more level but still vibrantly cheerful tone.

B-But {i}oh wow{/i}, I just can't believe it... When I got that parcel, I thought for sure that that was it, and I'd resigned myself to the idea that my story wouldn't make it outside of fan fiction, so... Talk about an amazing surprise, right!?

With your spinny dizziness now abating, and with Minoni having {i}mostly{/i} gotten her jubilant urges out of her system, you finally have an opening to voice a response, the bright-eyed monster girl staring at you with carefully contained eagerness as she awaits your input.
'I hope they're not just stringing you along...'

The big girl shows a rather conspicuous blush as she looks away in embarrassment before your forthright show of trust, most likely thinking back to her previous defeatism and not being very proud of it.
Y-Yeah, I guess I threw in the towel a bit quick, huh? Kinda unbecoming of me, after all the effort we put into making this happen...
So... Thank you, [ThePlayerName]. For believing in me, and for supporting me through the home stretch. I'm pretty sure that without your help, I'd have remained stuck in a rut to begin with.

She punctuates her earnest words with a gaze of undisguised, tender affection which once more shows clearly that her feelings towards you go much further than simply valuing you as an editor, the wordless demonstration making for a lovely, quiet moment that goes by warmly and softly, then eventually passes, Minoni's expression smoothing back into a more neutral smile as she awaits further comments on your part.

Minoni's reaction is instantaneous: as if she had been struck by a bolt of lightning, she starts and gazes at you with a dumbfounded stare, the minotaur proving so gobsmacked by your direct question that she can't even muster the presence of mine to deny it, instead frantically sputtering.

Then, in a flash of recollection, her eyes dart back to the letter still in her hand, and her eyes widen in understanding as she focuses on the initial 'Dear Chloe' that she seems to have completely glossed over in her previous state of dejected agitation, the deduction prompting a single, blank sound out of her.

I-I... hadn't caught that... I mean- I {i}did{/i} remember sending them my manuscript under my real name, because it felt more official, and also because I felt like it wouldn't have made sense to use an alias... but I didn't expect that they'd write back to me with a personally addressed letter and everything, y-you know?...

As she keeps rambling, however, she seems to realize that there's not much of a point to explaining {i}now{/i}, and gradually simmers down from her initial panic, her tizzied babbling trailing off into a quieter, calmer sigh of mild self-reproach that leaves her face scrunched up in a rueful pout as she directs her attention back on you and goes on in a more even tone.
But... yeah, it {i}is{/i} my real name. Kind of a lame reveal, huh...
I guess I {i}could{/i} have told you at some point, considering how close we are now, but it never felt like it could have come up naturally.
And also... I've {i}kinda{/i} grown used to 'Minoni'? ... I still think it's silly, but I've gotten more or less comfortable with it now... It's like a nickname, you know?
She seems to consider that a bit more, looking at you thoughtfully while fiddling with a lock of her hair in mulling hesitation, until she seems to make up her mind and goes on levelly.

And for that matter, I'd rather you keep calling me 'Minoni' when we're at the Temple... It would feel a bit weird to be called by my actual name when I'm effectively working, not to mention that it could get awkward if someone overhears us.
... But when we're on our own, feel free to call me 'Chloe' as much as you want.♥︎

Letting out a sweet little giggle after that flirtatious finish, she gives you a rather suggestive wink which attests to how comfortable she's gotten around you since you first met, her ensuing relaxed silence leaving the ball back in your court. Looks like you've unexpectedly learned something rather personal about her... though it doesn't seem like it's going to change your relationship all that much either.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'I hope they're not just stringing you along...'
theScene: The warily mentioned possibility sends a twinge of visible worry through Minoni, her eyes widening in resparked concern as she critically looks through the letter again, her body language and stare somewhat frantic as she stammers.
I-I'm {i}pretty{/i} sure they're not?! I-It would seem like a lot of unnecessary effort just to pull my leg...

As she speaks, she quickly leafs through her manuscript, which you can see has been annotated just as was said in the letter... and quite professionally at that, the comments and suggestions that you can see appearing to be made with informed care and with the same refined penmanship as the message, which quickly comforts the nervous authoress and calms her down, her frantic browsing abating to a decisive nod while she mumbles.
Yeah, that all seems legit to me... Frankly, I can't imagine who would have the kind of twisted sense of humor {i}and{/i} the kind of free time to pull off a 'joke' like that in the first place.

However, as she then goes back to perusing the letter with careful triple-checking attention, she squints perplexedly as her alert eyes scan the written lines, a hint of suspicion prompting her to muse pensively.

Though now that you mention it, those first few lines {i}do{/i} read like they were purposefully included to make me feel like it was all a dud before getting my hopes up again... I guess whoever was in charge of reviewing my manuscript was feeling... {i}mischievous{/i} or something?

She ponders the matter for a few moments more, but ultimately lets go of that train of thought with a shrug, looking like she's not going to lose sleep over it... Or at the very least, like she's going to {i}try{/i} not to.
The more you look at the letter's elegant calligraphy, the more you feel like you've already seen that kind of penmanship before, specifically from someone {i}very close{/i}...
music/Relaxing/Purple Planet Music - Gentle - Eastern Karma (2_16).mp3

Then it hits you: you remember that when you talked with Kotone about her newer writing, she showed you some of her work, and you're {i}pretty sure{/i} her handwriting is a dead ringer for the letter's, leaving you quite confident that she's the one who authored it, along with the detailed notes included with the manuscript.

Looking back to Minoni, you wonder what you should make of that, and whether you should tell her about it... but thinking more on it, you get the feeling that the revelation might further fray her already tense nerves after the emotional rollercoaster she's just been through... or that she might simply not believe you altogether.

Moreover, with the matter possibly involving confidentiality on the kitsune's part, you imagine that it would be better to first broach the topic with her, if only to better prepare your approach for when and if you want to tell your minotaur lover about it.

For the time being, with the aforementioned monster girl raising a questioning eyebrow at you in reaction to your musing silence, you return to the present and focus back on your ongoing conversation with her, shelving the Kotone considerations for later.
The query puts a glint of fervent enthusiasm in Minoni's gaze, the horned woman looking at you with fully reinvigorated energy as she confidently answers.

As you might have expected, she appears all ready to jump straight back into writing, but as she looks around and reminds herself that she is still at work, her gung-ho enthusiasm stalls and she hastily corrects herself.
N-Now, I'm gonna go back to doing my job as one of the Temple's proctors, and {i}not{/i} get distracted by my personal projects, ha ha...

Making a flustered and rather belated effort at being casual, she quickly stashes her manuscript, then takes her usual book back out, pointedly focusing on it as she reassumes her normal station by the door... though after glancing around in order to make sure there are neither visitors nor colleagues to catch her in the middle of a demonstration of possibly unprofessional behavior, she looks back to you and warmly smiles.

But... thank you, [ThePlayerName]. I know I must be kind of a broken record about it by now, but seriously, thank you. Without you, I would have never gotten that far. You've really helped stoke and channel my writing urges, and... um... {i}other{/i} kinds of urges, too.
As she speaks, she gives you a look that is {i}definitely{/i} unprofessional, swiftly looking around again before adding in a lower and positively lascivious tone of voice.
{size=-5}And just so you know, I would absolutely be in the mood to... {i}celebrate{/i} later. So if you're up for that, let me know, and we can go to my room and... have a moment.{/size}

And with those words, she somewhat hurriedly pretends to get absorbed in her book and in her proctorly duties, the horny lady leaving you to go back to your own devices with apparent detachment... which is starkly denied by the way that her tail whips happily behind her, betraying her glowing enthusiasm and her haste to go back to working on her manuscript... and also, most likely, her eager yearning to 'have a moment' with you.
Immediately and rather predictably, Minoni's cheeks flash a deep crimson at your brazen suggestion, her manuscript crinkling in her hands under the sudden, nervous tensing of her body.
But perhaps less predictably, she then fixes you with a look of definite, plain desire, the blush on her face abating to a lighter but no less warm flush as she unhesitantly answers.

And with that, she takes you by the hand and leads you towards her room with hurried steps. You can't but note that this sudden absence on her part may possibly count as professional misconduct, but this doesn't seem to be of concern to your minotaur lover who only has eyes for you, continuing to look at you with deep, eager affection while her hand stays tightly wrapped around yours all the way, her whole body thrumming with hot want.

As you make your way through the Temple's private quarters, you also notice that unlike your usual trips, you encounter no peeking and gossiping kunoichi trainees on the way, which may or may not be a coincidence...

Not that your enthusiastic guide seems to realize or care, focused as she is on the 'celebration' ahead as she marches through the wooden corridors with quick strides, until you've reached the entrance to her room which she hurriedly opens before stepping in alongside you, then swiftly closing the door behind the two of you, ensuring a modicum of privacy as she tosses her manuscript to her writing desk before facing you with overt thirst.
Now, let's see about those congratulations.♥︎
